channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a wide range of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This expansion enhances the capabilities of AI applications in real-world scenarios.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event is a key opportunity for tech enthusiasts to engage with upcoming innovations.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on experiences, emphasizing the transition to agentic AI. The event includes specialized tracks on various technical topics, enhancing developer skills.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, a new framework that significantly improves GPU and NPU acceleration for AI applications. This update also enhances support for PyTorch and JAX, marking a shift in TensorFlow's deployment capabilities.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour is a new initiative aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through interactive sessions. The program focuses on practical building and advanced AI topics.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to improve coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to make AI a more effective coding collaborator.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes features like Finish Changes and Outlines in IntelliJ and VS Code, designed to streamline coding by reducing manual prompting and improving code comprehension.

item.title: Amazon put a filesystem on S3; I showed up with a test suite and bad intentions
item.description: AWS has launched S3 Files, allowing users to mount S3 buckets as NFS shares. This new capability is being tested for its reliability and performance in real-world scenarios.

item.title: I asked 5 data leaders about how they use AI to automate - and end integration nightmares
item.description: Tech leaders discuss their strategies for automating data pipelines with AI, significantly reducing integration workloads. This shift marks a move away from outdated methods like Excel mapping.

item.title: Zephyr Energy loses £700K in cyber hit that rerouted contractor payment
item.description: Zephyr Energy has reported a cyber incident that resulted in a loss of £700,000 due to redirected contractor payments. The company is now working to recover the funds.

item.title: It's not a BIOS bug: How swapping to a DisplayPort 2.1b cable may fix your PC's boot loops
item.description: Swapping to a DisplayPort 2.1b cable can resolve boot loop issues in PCs, highlighting the impact of minor manufacturing errors on performance. This guidance helps users prevent potential problems.

item.title: UK.gov's top tech jobs pay more than prime minister earns
item.description: The UK government's Department for Science, Innovation and Technology is hiring directors general with salaries exceeding that of the Prime Minister, reflecting the high demand for tech leadership. This move underscores the importance of technology in government operations.

item.title: Capita's pension portal exposes civil servants' private data
item.description: Capita's pension portal briefly exposed the private data of civil servants, prompting a reduction in online functionality. This incident raises concerns about data security in public sector services.

item.title: The best business VoIP services in 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A guide to the best business VoIP services for 2026 has been released, helping businesses transition from traditional phone lines. This resource is essential for companies looking to modernize their communication systems.

item.title: Is fake grass a bad idea? The AstroTurf wars are far from over.
item.description: The debate over synthetic turf versus natural grass continues, as a new athletic field at Cornell University raises environmental concerns. This discussion reflects broader issues regarding sustainability in sports facilities.

item.title: Desalination technology, by the numbers
item.description: A detailed analysis of desalination technology reveals its growing importance in water-scarce regions, particularly in the Middle East. Understanding these statistics is crucial for addressing global water challenges.

item.title: UK to spend £15M on AI-powered crime mapping in knife violence crackdown
item.description: The UK government is investing £15 million in AI-powered crime mapping to combat knife violence, aiming to enhance targeted policing. This initiative reflects a growing reliance on technology in law enforcement.

item.title: This AI Wearable From Ex-Apple Engineers Looks Like an iPod Shuffle
item.description: A new AI wearable designed by former Apple engineers only activates when tapped, prioritizing user privacy. This innovation aims to address privacy concerns prevalent in many AI devices.

item.title: The best Hisense TVs of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best Hisense TVs for 2026 highlights the brand's rise in the mid-range and premium TV market. This guide is valuable for consumers seeking quality home entertainment options.

item.title: Microsoft software resale appeal catches eye of £3.5B class action
item.description: Microsoft's ongoing legal battle with ValueLicensing is entering an appeals phase, potentially impacting a £3.5 billion class action lawsuit. The outcome could have significant implications for software resale practices.

item.title: Sticky-note security turned gym into hall of '80s horrors
item.description: An article discusses how security credentials left unsecured can lead to mischief, highlighting vulnerabilities even in fitness setups. This serves as a reminder of the importance of proper security practices.

item.title: GitHub availability report: March 2026
item.description: GitHub's March 2026 availability report details four incidents that caused performance degradation across its services, affecting user experience. This highlights ongoing reliability challenges for the platform.

item.title: Why I stopped using 'Modern Standby' on my Windows laptop to save battery overnight
item.description: The article explains why using 'Modern Standby' on Windows laptops may not be the best choice for battery preservation overnight, suggesting alternative methods. This insight can help users optimize their device usage.

item.title: KDE Linux is the purest form of Plasma I've tested - but the install isn't for the meek
item.description: KDE Linux is praised for offering the purest form of the Plasma desktop environment, although the installation process may be challenging for less experienced users. This highlights the diversity in Linux distributions.

item.title: I found a hidden Google Photos tool that makes clearing storage feel less like a chore
item.description: A newly discovered tool in Google Photos allows users to easily swipe to keep or delete photos, streamlining the process of clearing storage, although it's currently available only on Android devices.

item.title: Copilot-reviewed pull request merge metrics now in the usage metrics API
item.description: The Copilot usage metrics API has been updated to include new metrics for pull requests reviewed by Copilot, building on previous metrics introduced in February. This enhances tracking of AI-assisted coding.

item.title: Outlook: Upgraded Video and Audio Player for iOS
item.description: Outlook's iOS app has received an upgrade with a new video and audio player that offers improved controls and playback experience. This update aims to enhance user engagement with multimedia content.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: AI-generated meeting archive for knowledge retention
item.description: Microsoft Teams is introducing an AI-generated meeting archive feature that allows retention of key insights from meetings, even after transcripts expire. This aims to enhance knowledge retention within organizations.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Entra passwordless resource account support for Teams Rooms on Windows devices
item.description: Microsoft Teams Rooms on Windows will support passwordless sign-in through Entra Resource Accounts, enhancing security and simplifying user management. This feature is set to improve the user experience for IT admins.

item.title: Secret scanning improvements to alert APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows
item.description: GitHub is rolling out improvements to its secret scanning features, enhancing alert systems for APIs, webhooks, and workflows. This aims to bolster security for developers using these tools.

item.title: Customize Amazon Nova models with Amazon Bedrock fine-tuning
item.description: Amazon Bedrock now allows users to fine-tune Amazon Nova models for specific tasks, enhancing model performance through better training data and hyperparameter optimization. This capability is significant for businesses needing tailored AI solutions.

item.title: Human-in-the-loop constructs for agentic workflows in healthcare and life sciences
item.description: AI agents in healthcare require human oversight to comply with regulations and ensure data sensitivity. The article discusses four approaches to implement human-in-the-loop systems effectively.

item.title: Building intelligent audio search with Amazon Nova Embeddings: A deep dive into semantic audio understanding
item.description: This post details the creation of an audio search system using Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings, explaining how audio can be represented as vectors for improved search capabilities. It provides practical code examples for implementation.

item.title: Reinforcement fine-tuning on Amazon Bedrock: Best practices
item.description: The article outlines best practices for reinforcement fine-tuning on Amazon Bedrock, using the GSM8K dataset as an example. It covers dataset preparation, reward function design, and hyperparameter tuning.

item.title: GitHub Mobile: Research and code with Copilot cloud agent anywhere
item.description: GitHub Mobile now allows users to utilize the Copilot cloud agent for research and coding on-the-go, enhancing productivity outside the traditional workspace. This feature expands Copilot's usability.

item.title: Code Security risk assessment available for organizations
item.description: GitHub has introduced a free Code Security risk assessment tool for organizations to identify and summarize security vulnerabilities, helping to enhance overall security posture.

item.title: GitHub Universe is back: We want you to take the stage
item.description: GitHub Universe is returning, inviting participants to share memorable sessions from past events, aiming to inspire and engage the developer community.

item.title: How Amazon uses agentic AI for vulnerability detection at global scale
item.description: Amazon's RuleForge system employs agentic AI to create detection rules for vulnerabilities at a much faster rate than traditional methods, showcasing efficiency in security processes.

item.title: New PGP signing key for GitHub CLI Linux packages
item.description: GitHub has released an updated PGP keyring for its CLI Linux packages, which includes both the current and a new signing key, ensuring secure package management.

item.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
item.description: The article discusses ALTK-Evolve, a framework for on-the-job learning for AI agents, emphasizing the importance of continuous learning in AI applications.

item.title: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, March Releases
item.description: GitHub has released updates for Visual Studio Code, introducing new features and improvements in its weekly stable releases. This enhances the development experience for users.

item.title: claude-mpm 6.2.25
item.description: The Claude Code framework has been updated to version 6.2.25, featuring multi-agent orchestration and enhanced session management for AI development. This update aims to streamline AI-powered workflows.

item.title: Anthropic rolls out cyber AI model days after source code leak - ft.com
item.description: Anthropic has launched its new cyber AI model, Project Glasswing, shortly after a source code leak. This model aims to identify zero-day vulnerabilities in major systems.

item.title: 1 May / Call for Submissions: Thresholds 55 – Property
item.description: MIT's Thresholds journal is calling for submissions for its upcoming issue focused on the concept of property, exploring its various implications. This provides a platform for academic discourse.

item.title: Small Wisconsin Town Rebukes Trump-Backed Project In Stunning Vote — With Famous Comedian’s Support
item.description: In a surprising vote, residents of a small Wisconsin town rejected a Trump-backed AI data center project, showcasing community resistance to AI infrastructure. This reflects growing concerns over AI's impact.

item.title: axiom-spec added to PyPI
item.description: The Axiom specification has been added to PyPI, allowing developers to compile .axiom specs into verified Python code using large language models. This enhances code reliability.

item.title: amfs-mcp-server 0.2.1
item.description: The AMFS MCP Server has been updated to version 0.2.1, enabling the exposure of Agent Memory as MCP tools for AI applications. This update enhances functionality for developers.

item.title: Grab an entire RTX 5090 gaming PC for just $8 more than the GPU itself and score a whopping $1,600 off — huge HP discount requires a $39 controller or monitor to secure you a 4K powerhouse with a 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5, and a 1TB SSD
item.description: HP is offering a significant discount on a gaming PC equipped with an RTX 5090, making it available for just $8 more than the GPU alone when bundled with certain accessories.

item.title: openai-agents-opentelemetry 0.2.2
item.description: OpenTelemetry has released an update for the OpenAI Agents SDK, enhancing tracing capabilities for developers. This update aims to improve monitoring and debugging.

item.title: WhatsApp adds a better, native interface for CarPlay
item.description: WhatsApp has improved its integration with CarPlay, providing users with a better native interface compared to the previous version. This enhances user experience while driving.
